page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n4 server family. It is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance features 8.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying Intel processor includes an L3 cache of 260 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 2 MB respectively, and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance profiles. The instance achieves average tier results in single-core metrics, with a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1684.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression at 28.28 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 11.93 GB/sec also perform in the average range. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show significant performance constraints. The multi-core stress-ng score of 1714.68 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2251.0 are categorized as poor and weak respectively. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10% at 19.35 GB/sec and 12.78 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the n4-standard-2 is positioned as an entry-level general-purpose instance. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight workloads that do not demand high memory throughput or sustained multi-threaded compute. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, light text processing, and simple single-threaded applications. It is less suitable for database operations, software compilation, or machine learning tasks, as evidenced by its weak Passmark database score of 1259.2 and poor LLM inference speeds on larger models.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical Intel core and 8.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on its Intel processor, which supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0 and solid single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core and memory-intensive workloads face performance limitations, as shown by a weak Geekbench multi-core score of 2251.0 and poor memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-efficient entry point for low-intensity, single-threaded applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight administrative tasks rather than database hosting, software compilation, or complex multi-threaded computing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It offers average single-core performance but faces multi-core and memory bandwidth limitations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced, entry-level virtual machine designed for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic general-purpose workloads without intensive memory demands.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture.
Powered by an Intel processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support.
Delivers average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Exhibits performance constraints in multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth.
Best suited for basic web serving, text processing, and light administrative tasks.
